feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Could 'humanmaxxing' actually help you live longer? Here's what experts say
entry.summary: Humanmaxxing is the latest wellness trend focused on maximizing human potential through biohacking, longevity science and advanced medical treatments.

entry.title: There’s an Easy Way to Reduce Summer Party Anxiety for Everyone. And We Already Have the Technology.
entry.summary: Do this at your next gathering—I beg you.

entry.title: Here are the at-home medical items nurses swear by
entry.summary: These health professionals rely on Vicks VapoRub, Benadryl, aloe vera gel and many other products to treat common ailments.

entry.title: 10 Signs You're Not Getting Enough Magnesium—and What to Do About It
entry.summary: Magnesium is an essential mineral, but many of us are missing the mark. Discover the warning signs of not getting enough magnesium and how to fix it.

entry.title: This rare tick-borne disease is on the rise in the U.S.
entry.summary: Cases of Powassan virus have hit the news in recent months because of the potentially life-altering, sometimes fatal, symptoms.

entry.title: The Best Tea for Lowering High Blood Pressure, Recommended by a Dietitian
entry.summary: Sipping tea is one way to help manage blood pressure naturally. Find out why green tea tops our list as the best choice for your heart.

entry.title: Here's What You Should Know If You Use Your Hands To Clean Your Body In The Shower
entry.summary: Doctors reveal the washing method thats REALLY best for your skin — and it might surprise you.

entry.title: Parasite That Affects Cognition Has Quietly Infected Billions of People
entry.summary: A parasite transmitted via cat turds has silently infected billions of people, and scientists are scrambling to contain it.

entry.title: Shingles vaccine may also offer protection against dementia, studies increasingly show
entry.summary: The shingles vaccine may significantly help protect older adults against dementia, a growing body of evidence shows

entry.title: Eggs score 10/10, quinoa just 4/10: Dietitian rates 9 popular protein sources and reveals which ones are worth eating | Health
entry.summary: From sprouted moong to Greek yoghurt, dietitian Shweta J Panchal rates nine protein-rich foods and reveals which ones deserve a place on your plate. | Health

entry.title: Endocrinologists Share 5 Habits to Break for Healthier Blood Sugar
entry.summary: Wondering how to support healthy blood sugar? Endocrinologists explain five common habits to rethink and what to do instead.

entry.title: Suspected brain cancer turns out to be pork tapeworm infestation
entry.summary: Doctors in Spain suspected that a man suffering from chronic headaches had brain cancer. A scan showed a number of poorly-defined lesions, typical of cancer that has spread from elsewhere…

entry.title: New vitamin B12 therapy shows promise against deadly brain cancer
entry.summary: Researchers have identified a vitamin B12–based compound that can cross the blood-brain barrier and home in on glioblastoma tumors. In animal studies, the compound accumulated preferentially in tumor tissue and delivered sustained nitric oxide directly to can…

entry.title: Arizona health officials investigate suspected pneumonic plague case
entry.summary: Yavapai County Community Health Services said Friday that a county resident is thought to have contracted the infection.

entry.title: Peptide alternative to antibiotics could combat antimicrobial resistance crisis
entry.summary: A University of Alberta research team has designed a promising alternative for treating antimicrobial-resistant infections, a pressing global health issue. In a paper recently published in Cell Biomaterials, the team describes preclinical testing results for …

entry.title: New magic mushroom makes users see tiny ‘gnome’ people — scientists have no idea how it’s doing that
entry.summary: Magic mushrooms? You have gnome idea.

entry.title: Infectious Information 6/27/2026
entry.summary: Vaccines and antibodies forge ahead despite certain idiocies. Including a new ACIP charter that de-emphasizes vaccines?! Ebola cases were near 900 on the 18th. They topped 1000 on the 22nd.

entry.title: Brooke Burke says 'menopause can be sexy'—here’s why
entry.summary: Brain fog, fatigue and more: Brooke Burke details her menopause journey and the Hone Health tools that helped her feel like herself again.

entry.title: Scratching that bug bite might feel good at first but science explains why it's a bad idea
entry.summary: You've likely heard it since childhood: Don't scratch that bug bite or rash, you'll make it worse. But why would something that feels so good be bad?

entry.title: Few sunscreens meet safety standards, new report says
entry.summary: Need to stock up on sunscreen? Few choices on store shelves today are both safe and effective, according to an annual report.

entry.title: Scientists discover what triggers belly fat as we age
entry.summary: Aging may trigger the appearance of specialized stem cells that supercharge the body's ability to create new belly fat. The discovery reveals a potential biological driver of middle-age weight gain and a promising target for future anti-obesity treatment…

entry.title: Raccoon with rabies scratches Manorville resident
entry.summary: Health department employees believe the animal was relocated to the area, possibly by someone else, according to the release.

entry.title: Parenting habit up to 6 months could protect kids against ADHD symptoms later
entry.summary: Parents, it’s time to focus up — your child’s attention span may depend on it.

entry.title: Cardiovascular Disease: 2 Hours Strength Training Lowers Risk by 20%
entry.summary: Researchers say that just 2 hours of weekly strength training may help lower the risk of cardiovascular disease in women, especially when paired with 150 minutes of weekly aerobic exercise.

entry.title: Jamestown Canyon virus detected in Exeter
entry.summary: State officials say Jamestown Canyon virus has been detected in a batch of mosquitoes in Exeter. The samples were collected June 16.

entry.title: The #1 Snack for Better Gut Health, Recommended by a Gastroenterologist
entry.summary: Looking to improve your gut health? Read on to discover the best gastroenterologist-recommended plant-based snack to add to your day.

entry.title: Large MRI analysis uncovers brain-region thinning tied to depression
entry.summary: Major depressive disorder (MDD) is a psychiatric condition characterized by persistent feelings of sadness, a loss of interest in everyday activities, altered sleeping and/or eating patterns, low energy, and difficulty concentrating on tasks. While it is one …

entry.title: These leafy greens could help protect your lungs, study suggests
entry.summary: Researchers found that higher vitamin K1 intake from leafy greens like spinach and kale was associated with a 16% lower likelihood of COPD over ten years.

entry.title: Hidden Virus May Have Infected 9.4 Million People – Scientists Say We’ve Missed Most Cases
entry.summary: New research indicates the Oropouche virus has spread far more widely than reported, exposing major gaps in disease surveillance.

entry.title: Scratching that bug bite might feel good at first, but science explains why it's a bad idea
entry.summary: You scratched that bug bite, and now it's a big, itchy bump. Some science explains why scratching really can make things worse, even if you don't break the skin.

entry.title: 20 No-Sugar Mediterranean Diet Recipes for Beginners
entry.summary: These no-sugar-added recipes seamlessly align with the Mediterranean diet. Plus, they come together in 15 minutes of active time or less.

entry.title: Fish Oil vs. Cod Liver Oil Supplements: Which Is Better for Heart Health?
entry.summary: Fish oil and cod liver oil supplements provide essential nutrients. Learn which is more effective for supporting heart health.

entry.title: New blood test detects 90% of aggressive prostate cancer cases, beating current screenings
entry.summary: Stockholm3 blood test detected 90% of aggressive prostate cancer cases compared to 74% for PSA tests in a study of over 12,000 men in Sweden.

entry.title: Louisiana Notches a Sickle Cell First
entry.summary: 23-year-old is first in state to be 'functionally cured' of sickle cell disease

entry.title: Green bananas aren't necessarily bad — experts explain the hidden health perk
entry.summary: Food experts explain why some bananas never ripen properly, citing immature harvesting and chilling injury during storage as the key factors.

entry.title: We asked Bay Area residents where they'd like to move. The answers surprised us
entry.summary: Readers told us the Bay Area cities where they'd like to move. Their unexpected choices expressed a love for fog, frugality, public transit and … pie?

entry.title: Scratching that bug bite might feel good at first but science explains why it’s a bad idea
entry.summary: You scratched that bug bite and now it's a big itchier bump. Some science explains why scratching really can make things worse, even if you don't break the skin. University of Pittsburgh researchers put tiny “cones of shame” onto mice to help unravel what hap…

entry.title: Girl, 11, boards plane with mystery symptoms—man beside her changes everything
entry.summary: Caitlin Neelley told Newsweek: “We never got his name and never saw him again.“

entry.title: U.S. may have lost a ‘crown jewel’ of public health, experts say
entry.summary: The U.S. eliminated measles in 2000. But experts now say ongoing outbreaks have probably ended one of the nation’s biggest public health successes.

entry.title: The Most Common Warning Signs Of Rectal Cancer
entry.summary: Rates of rectal cancer deaths are rising in adults under 50.

entry.title: Health Experts Reveal The 1 Thing They Wish Their Older Patients Would Do
entry.summary: Here are a few small ways to do yourself some major favors, from pros like cardiologists and nutritionists.

entry.title: Hospital Opens Roof Garden Where Critical Care Patients Can Enjoy the Outdoors for Hours With Full Care
entry.summary: A hospital in London opened a garden on its roof where Critical Care patients can spend hours in the fresh air and sunlight.

entry.title: If a Lyme disease vaccine gets approved, how would it go over? We asked hunters
entry.summary: Drugmakers are working on a potential new shot to prevent the tick-borne illness. How might it fare in the era of vaccine skepticism?

entry.title: “An agonising way to die: it causes whole body convulsions, and the victim is painfully and horribly aware…”
entry.summary: Also called the Asian vomit button tree, the strychnine tree contains one of nature’s deadliest poisons, famously featured in many mystery novels.

entry.title: Weekend reads: A tsunami of misleading medical studies; retraction calls cancer therapy timing into question; a closer look at Max Planck’s retractions
entry.summary: If your week flew by — we know ours did — catch up here with what you might have missed. The week at Retraction Watch featured: Springer Nature to start issuing expressions of concern for books A p…

entry.title: 7-Day No-Sugar Mediterranean Diet Meal Plan for Beginners, Created by a Dietitian
entry.summary: This simple no-added-sugar Mediterranean diet meal plan for beginners features recipes that require no more than 15 minutes of active cooking time.

entry.title: A chef-turned nutrition scientist eats 30g of fiber a day. She shared her go-to breakfast, lunch, and dinner.
entry.summary: You may eat fiber for gut health, but it plays many crucial roles in the body, from lowering cholesterol to helping to control glucose levels.

entry.title: A cardiologist said 3 foods that seem healthy could be harming your heart
entry.summary: A cardiologist said popular "healthy" foods can actually be bad for your heart, since they're highly processed and full of additives like sugar and fat.

entry.title: New procedure treats hard-to-manage high blood pressure
entry.summary: St. Anne's Hospital performs first renal denervation procedure to treat high blood pressure in Southeastern Massachusetts.

entry.title: RI man contracts rare tick-borne ‘Powassan’ virus
entry.summary: A Rhode Island man is recovering after contracting a rare tick-borne disease, according to the Rhode Island Department of Health.
